warnings: No rotatable dihedrals detected (or supplied) — nothing to scan. For rigid molecules use --dihedral i,j,k,l to force a scan.

prose:
Conformational analysis of hydrogen peroxide (H–O–O–H) was attempted with the chemkit conformational-analysis skill at the GFN2-xTB level (GFN2-xTB tight-binding Hamiltonian; Bannwarth et al., JCTC 2019).

• Property: relaxed dihedral scan → rotation barrier(s) per detected rotatable bond
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (xtb backend as bundled with chemkit; no Gaussian basis)
• Geometry provenance: the provided input geometry (/Users/evane/…/hydrogen-peroxide.xyz) was taken as-is; no pre-optimization requested.
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Scan protocol: autodetected rotors (default), 24 steps over 360 °.
• Software: xtb via chemkit MCP wrapper.
• Convergence: not applicable — the engine detected no rotatable dihedrals and performed no scans.
• Warnings (verbatim): “No rotatable dihedrals detected (or supplied) — nothing to scan. For rigid molecules use --dihedral i,j,k,l to force a scan.”
• Live log path: given in the cli_invocation, streamed during execution.

Result: the engine’s rotor detection classified hydrogen peroxide as having no rotatable bonds under default heuristics, so **no rotation barrier could be computed**. To obtain the H–O–O–H torsional barrier, rerun specifying that dihedral explicitly (e.g. `--dihedral 3,1,2,4` using the atom indices from the .xyz file).
